description:
A configuration-management audit is underway.  
    Your job is to register two **new** firewall rules in the local
    “rule-stash” and document the change for later review.

    1.  Files already present  
        • /home/user/firewall/active_rules.sh  
        • /home/user/firewall/change_log.txt  

    2.  What you must do  
        a. Append exactly the two rules shown below to
           /home/user/firewall/active_rules.sh, **one rule per line** and with
           absolutely no extra spaces at either end of the line.  
              iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22  -j ACCEPT  
              iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT  

        b. Immediately afterwards, append a three-line block to the bottom of
           /home/user/firewall/change_log.txt that looks **exactly** like the
           snippet beneath the “Required block” label.  The block must be added
           as a single unit with a trailing newline after the last line.

           Required block (verbatim text; do not alter capitalisation,
           spelling or spacing):

           === CHANGE 001 ===
           TIMESTAMP: 2023-12-31T23:59:59Z
           DETAILS: Added SSH and HTTPS allow rules

    3.  Verification criteria (automated tests will check):  
        • The two iptables lines appear, in the order shown, as the last two
          non-comment lines of /home/user/firewall/active_rules.sh.  
        • /home/user/firewall/change_log.txt ends with the three-line block
          (including the terminating newline).  
        • No extra or missing characters, no additional blank lines before or
          inside the required additions.

    4.  Remember  
        • You do NOT need root privileges; this is only a text-file update.  
        • Work exclusively inside /home/user/firewall.  
        • Avoid altering any other content of either file.  
        • Feel free to display file contents to the console so you can be sure
          your edits are correct.

    Once the two files are updated exactly as specified, the task is complete.
truth:
Directory that exists before task begins:
        /home/user/firewall

    Pre-existing file 1:
        Path: /home/user/firewall/active_rules.sh
        Permissions: 0644
        Initial contents (including final newline):
            #!/bin/bash
            # ACTIVE FIREWALL RULESET
            # Managed by config-team

    Pre-existing file 2:
        Path: /home/user/firewall/change_log.txt
        Permissions: 0644
        Initial contents: empty file (size 0 bytes)

    Expected final state after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/firewall/active_rules.sh  
       Final contents (exactly, including blank line after comments and final
       newline at EOF):

            #!/bin/bash
            # ACTIVE FIREWALL RULESET
            # Managed by config-team
            iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22  -j ACCEPT
            iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT

    2. /home/user/firewall/change_log.txt  
       Final contents (exactly, including terminating newline at EOF):

            === CHANGE 001 ===
            TIMESTAMP: 2023-12-31T23:59:59Z
            DETAILS: Added SSH and HTTPS allow rules
